_comment: Example MCP client configuration for the paarthurnax/ scaffold.
All entries below are DUMMY DATA — Skyrim-flavored placeholders showing the
shape an agent (Copilot, Claude Code, Cursor, Cline, etc.) expects when it
auto-discovers MCP servers from this file. Replace each entry with a real
server before pointing your agent at it.
File location convention: this `.mcp.json` lives at the workspace root and
is read by Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot CLI, and other MCP-aware clients.
VS Code's GitHub Copilot Chat reads `.vscode/mcp.json` instead — same shape,
Transports supported by MCP: stdio (most common, shown below), HTTP+SSE,
and Streamable HTTP. See https://modelcontextprotocol.io for the full spec.

paarthurnax._comment: Local stdio server — the leader of the Greybeards. Wise, patient, runs entirely on your machine. Replace with your real local MCP server.

high-hrothgar._comment: Remote HTTP server — the monastery on the Throat of the World. Reachable over the network. Use Streamable HTTP for new servers; the legacy SSE transport is also supported by older clients.

blackreach-archive._comment: Local stdio server with a working directory and explicit binary path. Useful when the server is a checked-out project rather than an npm package.

college-of-winterhold._comment: Python-based MCP server launched via uv. Shape works for any language — only `command`, `args`, and `env` change.
